headline: Home tech to soup up your dinner – and your security
summary: A multi-menu robochef and other domestic helpers
body_text:
In the dialect of Reggio Emilia, a town midway between Parma and Bologna, the word liffo can mean two things: either a person who really enjoys their food, or food that’s particularly delicious. For two Italian tech entrepreneurs, Stefano Pedrazzi and Massimiliano Delsante, the word sums up a love of regional gastronomy, and it’s also the name they’ve given to a unique cooking robot that, they believe, will redefine the domestic kitchen.

Already shipping in Italy and shortly coming to the US and later to the UK, it’s essentially a one-pot automated chef: choose a recipe from a list of more than 200, load up the appropriate ingredients into eight 500g-capacity containers inside the machine, fill its water tank, set the mealtime and forget about it. (An internal cooling system effectively refrigerates the food while it’s waiting, so it can be loaded and programmed up to 24 hours in advance.)
